summary: This is a brief, humorous anecdote about the author and a co-worker visiting a colleague's house to pick something up for work. While demoing a new HD television, they happened to catch a Maury Povich episode about someone with a fear of mustard and pickles. Atwood frames this coincidental viewing as a 'Zen moment' and uses it as a reminder that things could always be worse.
key_points: Serendipitous moments can offer unexpected perspective
The contrast between cutting-edge technology (HD TV) and lowbrow content (trash TV) creates humor
Perspective on one's own problems can come from the most unlikely sources
money_quotes: It could be worse. Your fear of mustard and pickles could be ruining your life.
key_insight: Sometimes the most trivial encounters remind us that our problems, however frustrating, pale in comparison to life's more absurd struggles.
